intro: The clearest split is that fd has a free tier while Coveo does not. Coveo is AI-powered search and recommendations platform for enterprise portals, e-commerce, and support sites; fd is CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Fast Find. The data points Coveo at e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search, and fd at automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Pricing: Coveo is subscription; fd is free, free tier. Lock-in is high for Coveo and low for fd, with DX at 4/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 2/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Coveo emphasises e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search, and fd emphasises automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Only Coveo lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: Coveo is not for simple projects; fd is not for hobby projects, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Coveo is for e-commerce; fd is for mobile; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Coveo if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is simple projects.
verdict_b: Choose fd if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is hobby projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for Coveo and unknown for fd. fd offers a free tier while Coveo does not, so initial cost favors fd. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Coveo to fd?
faq.a: Coveo is tagged with high lock-in (enterprise search platform, hard to migrate). fd is tagged with low lock-in (oss cli find replacement). At least one side is high lock-in, so budget migration time for re-implementing integrations.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Coveo or fd?
faq.a: The dataset rates fd at 5/5 for developer experience and Coveo at 4/5, so fd scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is fd a good alternative to Coveo?
faq.a: fd is listed as fitting mobile, which overlaps with but is not identical to Coveo's scope. fd is flagged as not for hobby projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Coveo to fd means gaining access to a free tier.
